<local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

<bash-stdout>diff --git a/ghostty/config b/ghostty/config
index a86fd33..b94af00 100644
--- a/ghostty/config
+++ b/ghostty/config
@@ -155,6 +155,10 @@ gtk-titlebar = false
 # Auto-update check
 auto-update = check
 
+# Session Restoration
+# Save and restore windows, tabs, and splits on restart
+window-save-state = always
+
 # Confirm before closing windows with running processes
 confirm-close-surface = true</bash-stdout><bash-stderr></bash-stderr>
is this setting for ghostty working or not? Make sure this configuration via context7.

content.text:
### Action: toggle_visibility

Source: https://ghostty.org/docs/config/keybind/reference

Shows or hides all Ghostty windows. Only implemented on macOS.

```APIDOC
## ACTION toggle_visibility

### Description
Show or hide all windows. If windows are shown, Ghostty is focused. If hidden, focus is yielded to the OS.

### Method
INTERNAL_ACTION

### Platform
macOS only

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **status** (string) - Success message indicating window visibility state change.
```

--------------------------------

### Action: toggle_quick_terminal

Source: https://ghostty.org/docs/config/keybind/reference

Toggles the visibility of the 'Quake-style' drop-down terminal. This terminal state is preserved between toggles.

```APIDOC
## ACTION toggle_quick_terminal

### Description
Toggle the quick terminal (drop-down/Quake-style). The terminal state is preserved between appearances.

### Method
INTERNAL_ACTION

### Parameters
None

### Request Example
{
  "action": "toggle_quick_terminal"
}

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **status** (string) - Success message indicating the terminal has been toggled.
```

--------------------------------

### Configure vertical window padding

Source: https://ghostty.org/docs/config/reference

Sets the vertical padding between terminal cells and window borders in points. Supports single values for uniform padding or comma-separated values for distinct top and bottom padding.

```Ghostty Config
window-padding-y = 2
window-padding-y = 2,4
```

### Configuration > window-save-state

Source: https://ghostty.org/docs/config/reference

The `window-save-state` option controls whether Ghostty saves and restores the window state, including position, size, tabs, and splits. Some aspects of window state, like preserving working directories, require shell integration. The valid values are `default`, `never`, and `always`. `default` uses the system's default behavior, which on macOS typically saves state only on forced termination or systemwide configuration. `never` prevents any window state saving, and `always` saves state every time Ghostty is exited. Changing this setting while Ghostty is not running can affect the next launch's window state restoration. The default value is `default`. This feature is currently only supported on macOS.

--------------------------------

### Configuration > Reloading the Configuration

Source: https://ghostty.org/docs/config

The configuration can be reloaded at runtime by pressing `ctrl+shift+,` (Linux) or `cmd+shift+,` (macOS). These are the default keybindings, but it is possible to define your own via the `reload_config` action.
Some configuration options cannot be reloaded at runtime; others may only apply to newly created terminals. See the specific configuration option for details.

```Ghostty Config
window-padding-y = 2
```

### Configuration > window-save-state

Source: https://ghostty.org/docs/config/reference

Some aspects of window state, like preserving working directories, require shell integration. Changing this setting while Ghostty is not running can affect the next launch's window state restoration. The default value is `default`. This feature is currently only supported on macOS.

--------------------------------

Some configuration options cannot be reloaded at runtime; others may only apply to newly created terminals. See the specific configuration option for details.

content.text:
The `window-save-state = always` setting is **valid and working**. Here's what the Ghostty docs confirm:

- **Valid values**: `default`, `never`, `always`
- **`always`**: saves window state (position, size, tabs, splits) every time Ghostty exits, and restores on next launch
- **Platform**: macOS only (which matches your setup)
- **Note**: Some aspects like preserving working directories require shell integration

Your configuration is correct. One thing to be aware of: if you change this setting while Ghostty is **not running**, it can affect whether state is restored on the next launch.

574a2c5 docs(secrets): update secret management section with caching details
b37a368 feat(secrets): cache 1Password secrets with 24h TTL to reduce Touch ID prompts
e15be7e fix(secrets): correct 1Password vault name from Personal to Private
2ee3d06 feat(secrets): add Gemini API key and migrate to Personal vault

input.command:
git add ghostty/config && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(ghostty): enable session restoration on restart

Save and restore windows, tabs, and splits by setting
window-save-state to always.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
